headline: Took CIDER's paid media in-house and tripled performance
context: Rebuilt Naver & Kakao paid media end to end after agency results fell ~70%.

headline: Reworked CIDER's Naver KOL playbook for +67% GMV
context: Built sponsorship guidelines, an influencer tier chart, and metrics from scratch.

detail.overview: CIDER's influencer marketing on Naver ran without structure, internal SOPs, or reliable tracking.
detail.problem: Naver results are hard to track directly, and there was no repeatable playbook, so each campaign started from zero.
actions: Wrote detailed sponsorship guidelines so influencers produced varied, on-brand content
Built an influencer tier chart and defined performance metrics in Excel
Followed up individually with each blogger to recover campaign results Naver couldn't track
Negotiated using cross-channel mirrored exposure instead of just higher fees
impact: +67% GMV versus the previous KOL campaign
Repeatable SOPs and tiering adopted across Naver, Instagram, and YouTube

headline: Built CIDER's ambassador program to 9x sales on budget
context: Ran it end to end as PM — 20+ styled looks, tiered rewards, ad-ready content.

detail.overview: CIDER, a fast-fashion e-commerce brand, had little local awareness or loyalty in the Korea market.
detail.problem: The brand needed deeper engagement, a reason for local shoppers to explore the app, and a steady supply of advertising content.
actions: Designed the program around three goals: engagement, an in-app exclusive collection, and ad content
Prepared 20+ head-to-toe styling looks, finalized through manager and team feedback
Coordinated with logistics for a final check two weeks before each shoot
Set reward tiers across categories (most reviews, most content, best reels) and routed content to ad and design teams
impact: Sales reached 9x the invested budget
Content hit 52% for feed and 47% for reels, surpassing initial targets

headline: Ran CIDER's Seoul pop-up to 3x competitors' footfall
context: Led event, viral marketing, press, and agency contracts on a lean budget.

detail.overview: Ahead of pop-ups at Waterbomb and Konkuk University, CIDER launched a large pop-up at Space Wadiz, Seoul.
detail.problem: The event had to make a big splash on a tight budget, and offline pop-up ROI is hard to measure.
actions: Sent well-written press drafts to a curated journalist list to earn coverage without paid placement
Researched other pop-up stores for format and merchandising ideas
Built a data-tracking framework and aligned KPIs across teams
Worked through ROI from viral marketing, offline footfall, and SNS participation
impact: +7,243% surge in SNS search volume
About 3x the venue's usual visitor rate, and 3x competitors' average footfall

headline: Grew CIDER's Naver Blog +81% as the brand entered Korea
context: Built Instagram-style content and ran interim customer service before support existed.

detail.overview: With no recognition in Korea, CIDER needed reach and an early customer-service outlet before formal support existed.
detail.problem: Limited manpower, no brand awareness, and rising customer questions all landed at once — and the blog had to feel like an extension of the brand.
actions: Engaged influencers directly by commenting on their blogs to build relationships
Created Instagram-style Naver content (trend roundups, styling guides, vacation picks)
Posted idol-inspired coordinated looks on Twitter to reach style-driven audiences
Ran exchange, return, sizing, and FAQ posts as interim customer service
impact: +81% daily visitors to the Naver Blog
Follower count doubled within six months

headline: Lifted CIDER's address conversion +20% with localized UX
context: Specced an auto-address API, reworked sizing, and mapped the full refund journey.

detail.overview: CIDER's app lacked Korean localization and local-standard UX for sizing, address entry, and returns.
detail.problem: Foreign-centric sizing and a manual address flow created friction for Korean shoppers, and changes needed C-level buy-in.
actions: Wrote the dev document for an automatic address-fill service and integrated it with the backend team
Ran an A/B test with and without the address API
Benchmarked domestic e-commerce sizing and proposed localized options
Mapped the full return/refund journey stage by stage against competitors and surfaced weak points
impact: +20% address conversion from the automatic address API
Localized sizing reduced related customer inquiries
Leadership accepted the gaps and moved forward on improvements

headline: Localized CIDER's CRM on KakaoTalk to lift retention
context: Integrated Kakao Alimtalk and a chatbot, and wrote CS SOPs for overseas teams.

detail.overview: CIDER's CRM relied only on email and in-app push, with no localized channel — while Korean e-commerce leans heavily on KakaoTalk.
detail.problem: There was no localized CRM channel, and no Korean-language customer service for local shoppers.
actions: Integrated Kakao Alimtalk messages: payment reminder (30 min), split shipping, order confirmation, review request
Synced templates with the internal tech team
Planned and built a Kakao customer-service chatbot and wrote localized CS SOPs for overseas teams
Mapped 10+ competitors across fashion e-commerce, fashion brands, and SPA brands for comparison
impact: Higher retention and payment-success rates after the Kakao messages went live
Improved local CS quality through SOPs
Chatbot reduced direct inquiries and collected complaint data

headline: Turned CIDER customer FGIs into a product action plan
context: Planned and ran focus group interviews end to end as PM.

detail.overview: As a customer-centric team, CIDER needed real Voice of the Customer from domestic users to guide product and marketing.
detail.problem: There was no structured way to capture in-depth feedback on UI/UX and product from Korean customers.
actions: Planned and ran focus group interviews end to end as PM
Collected detailed feedback on UI/UX and product offerings
Synthesized findings into a prioritized action plan
impact: Customer feedback turned into a concrete, prioritized action plan for the team

headline: Modeled a multinational's market share in Power BI
context: Portfolio project on a sample dataset — Power Query cleaning and DAX modeling.

detail.overview: A portfolio project analyzing a multinational electronics manufacturer (VanArsdel) against competitors using a sample benchmarking dataset.
detail.problem: Five separate tables (sales, products, manufacturers, geography, dates) needed cleaning and modeling before any insight was possible.
actions: Cleaned and reshaped data in Power Query (fill-down, split columns, append, conditional columns, type fixes)
Built table relationships and a date table, and hid unnecessary tables
Wrote DAX measures: % Growth, PY Sales, total Sales, and Market Share
Used grouping to show only the top competitors and added filter-based interactions
impact: Mapped VanArsdel at 48.55% market share with 10.94% average growth (2017-2021)
Surfaced that the US drove 52.73% of revenue while Canada was underpenetrated — a localization opportunity

headline: Found the drivers behind a 26.86% telecom churn rate
context: Portfolio project — Excel pivot analysis across demographics, plans, and geography.

detail.overview: A portfolio project analyzing customer churn for a US telecom (Databel sample dataset) to surface drivers and propose actions.
detail.problem: Churn was rising in a competitive market, but the reasons weren't broken down by who was leaving or why.
actions: Cleaned the data using a structured checklist and built a churn-rate column for pivot analysis
Categorized churn reasons and grouped ages into Under 30 / Senior / Other
Cross-analyzed churn by data plan, contract length, and state with international activity
impact: Quantified overall churn at 26.86%, mostly lost to competitors with better devices/offers
Isolated high-risk segments: seniors, unlimited-plan users under 5GB, and international users in California

headline: Built an interactive Excel/VBA sales dashboard
context: Portfolio project — 5,782 rows with slicers, VBA controls, and theme switching.

detail.overview: A portfolio project to push Excel data-visualization skills, building an interactive dashboard from a US company's sales data.
detail.problem: A 5,782-row dataset across 10 categories needed to be explorable by non-technical viewers, not just static charts.
actions: Built pivot-based waterfall, stacked bar, line, map, combo, and doughnut charts
Used GETPIVOTDATA for real-time text callouts and added slicers for filtering
Coded VBA interactive buttons, info tabs, color-theme switching, and on/off toggles
impact: Delivered a fully interactive dashboard covering revenue, delivery, returns, and satisfaction
Surfaced clear reads like 67% on-time delivery and a 10% return rate

headline: Segmented customers into 5 actionable groups in Python
context: Portfolio project — compared K-Means, DBSCAN, PCA, and GMM; chose a 5-cluster GMM.

detail.overview: A portfolio project building a customer-segmentation model on a public marketing dataset so campaigns could be targeted by behavior.
detail.problem: A heterogeneous customer base had heavy overlaps, so raw charts gave no clean segments to act on.
actions: Ran feature engineering (grouped age, marital status, family size, expenses, engagement) and dropped outliers
Compared K-Means, hierarchical clustering, DBSCAN, PCA, and GMM
Selected a 5-cluster GMM for finer granularity at an acceptable silhouette score (0.1342)
Mapped each cluster to a marketing play (discounts, BNPL, VIP, cashback, household deals)
impact: Produced 5 clearly defined, action-ready customer segments
Confirmed a positive income-to-spend relationship to prioritize high-value targets

headline: Analyzed 39 job posts to rank in-demand analyst skills
context: Self-collected 39 international Tokyo postings and analyzed them in Excel.

detail.overview: A self-directed project analyzing what international employers in Tokyo actually expect from data and business analysts.
detail.problem: Skill expectations were anecdotal — there was no data-backed view of what to prioritize learning.
actions: Collected 39 Data/Business Analyst postings, focused on international companies
Coded and counted required skills and tools in Excel
Ranked programming languages and visualization tools by frequency
impact: Found SQL required most often (ahead of Python/R), with visualization second — Tableau leading, then Power BI, Excel, Qlik, Looker
Confirmed bilingual ability and a bachelor's degree as baseline, with domain knowledge and communication as differentiators

headline: Test Entry
context: Placeholder record for system testing.

detail.overview: This is a test entry.

headline: Built Full-Funnel AMC Measurement Framework for a CPG Pet Food Brand in Japan
context: Designed and engineered an end-to-end Amazon Marketing Cloud pipeline—covering SQL, data loading, and visualization—to measure campaign penetration across Awareness, Consideration, Purchase, and Loyalty for a Japan market CPG client at a global media agency.

detail.overview: Designed and built a full-funnel penetration measurement framework for a CPG pet food brand in Japan. The pipeline ran from AMC SQL queries through BigQuery storage to Python visualizations, covering four product categories (Standard Diet and Prescription Diet, Dog and Cat) across four funnel stages.
detail.problem: The client needed a single, consistent view of how many unique users were reached and converted at each funnel stage—from DSP impressions through Subscribe-and-Save loyalty orders—across multiple product lines. No existing framework handled AMC's data constraints, Japan timezone requirements, and brand-consistent reporting simultaneously.
actions: Wrote 300+ line multi-CTE AMC SQL queries using dsp_impressions, conversions_all (Flexible Shopping Insights), and APAC audience segment tables to measure reach and conversion across all funnel stages.
Built an ASIN mapping CTE covering 240+ product ASINs across four categories, and implemented cumulative quarterly reach logic so that Trend totals matched Overall totals for stakeholder clarity.

Diagnosed and resolved three critical data issues: a cumulative window mismatch between Overall and Trend views, a 13-month AMC data retention gap removing Q1 2025, and a tracked_asin vs tracked_item join error that caused Consideration metrics to return zero rows.
Loaded query outputs into Google BigQuery with a standardized table naming convention, authenticated via Google Cloud SDK in Colab.
Built a reusable Python visualization toolkit (style guide v3.2) generating donut pie and line charts in brand colors, exported as PNG (300 dpi) and PDF (vector).

Documented an AMC SQL parser bug where aliases matching source column names caused silent aggregation errors in UNION ALL contexts; established a safe-prefix naming convention (raw_, u_, conv_, agg_, sum_) to prevent recurrence.
impact: Delivered a mathematically consistent full-funnel penetration report across four product categories, with Overall and Trend metrics fully aligned.
Produced 10 visualization assets (4 funnel stages × Overall + Trend, PNG + PDF) and a reusable toolkit applicable to future campaigns.
Created an AMC SQL best-practices guide documenting a previously undocumented parser bug, reducing risk of silent data errors in future AMC projects at the agency.
skills: AMC SQL (multi-CTE, window functions, cumulative aggregation, timezone handling)
Google BigQuery (data loading, table management, GCP authentication)
Data pipeline engineering (AMC → BigQuery → Python → Visualization)

headline: Frequency Cap Removal Cut Reach by 26% While Raising Cost-per-Reach 9%
context: Ran a pre/post quasi-experiment in Amazon Marketing Cloud to assess whether removing the 6x/month frequency cap on a premium whisky brand's Awareness line items improved media efficiency.

detail.overview: Analyzed the impact of uncapping impression frequency on Awareness campaigns for a premium whisky brand using AMC SQL. Equalized 14-day pre/post windows to isolate frequency effects from time-accumulation bias.
detail.problem: The media team removed a 6x/month frequency cap to test whether relaxing delivery constraints would improve reach and lower CPM. The actual effect on cost-per-reach was unknown.
actions: Designed a pre/post quasi-experiment with matched 14-day windows in AMC SQL to control for frequency time-accumulation bias.
Mapped funnel stages (Awareness / Consideration / Purchase / Loyalty) via line item naming conventions (AW/CON/PUR/LO).
Aggregated privacy-safe unique reach using COUNT DISTINCT user_id inside CTEs per AMC clean room rules.
Bucketed users by frequency saturation level to identify where repeat exposure was concentrated.
Derived CPM and CPR for both windows and calculated directional change.
impact: Uncapping doubled average frequency (4.33→8.50) while reducing unique reach by 26% — cheaper CPM inventory was absorbed by repeat exposure rather than incremental reach.
Recommended reinstating the 6x/month cap; projected roughly 2x improvement in CPR if impressions are redistributed at capped frequency.

headline: Funnel Penetration Analytics & Visualization Standard for a Global Pet Nutrition Brand's APCL
context: Built end-to-end funnel penetration charts and a reusable visualization standard for a global pet nutrition brand's APCL reporting, via a media agency; sole analyst and documentation owner.

detail.overview: Developed monthly funnel penetration visualizations (Awareness / Consideration / Purchase / Loyalty) across Dog/Cat × SD/PD segments, and authored a team-wide visualization standard to keep outputs PPT-ready and brand-consistent.
detail.problem: Funnel reporting lacked a consistent visual standard, required BigQuery table access to run, and had no documentation—making it hard to reuse or hand off. Funnel data also showed a reach imbalance that needed a clear recommendation.
actions: Refactored BigQuery-dependent pipeline into a portable in-memory DataFrame (StringIO/read_csv) with NaN-aware handling for missing segment data
Designed a brand color system encoding two dimensions (species + tier) without a legend: Red=Dog/Blue=Cat, vivid=SD/deep=PD
Authored a Pet Food Python Visualization Guide v1.0, covering PowerPoint export specs (transparent PNG/SVG, Calibri/Malgun Gothic, 16:9 sizing)
Wrote a neutral-tone strategic budget recommendation (EN/KR) against a 70% reach benchmark, proposing three reallocation paths including AMC audience expansion
impact: Standardized chart output cuts rework and ensures brand-consistent, PPT-ready visuals across the team
Identified funnel imbalance—Awareness and Purchase over-indexed while Consideration and Loyalty under-indexed—and proposed three concrete budget optimization paths

headline: Built Unified AMC Pipeline for Sponsored Ads KPI & Media Cost — Fixed SQL Accuracy Issues
context: Designed and debugged an AMC clean room SQL pipeline consolidating KPI and spend across all Sponsored Ads campaign types (SP/SB/SD/STV); established team SQL conventions.

detail.overview: In Amazon Marketing Cloud's clean room environment, built a single-query pipeline that joins KPI data (attributed events) with media cost (CPC + VCPM) for all Sponsored Ads campaign types, and implemented a multi-level campaign classification system. Simultaneously diagnosed and resolved integer division truncation and alias conflict bugs that were corrupting ratio metrics.
detail.problem: Ratio KPIs (NTB rate, AOV, etc.) were returning incorrect values due to integer division truncation inside multi-level CTE + UNION/JOIN structures. Column alias conflicts made root-cause diagnosis slow. A missing classification catch-all caused a growing 'Other' bucket, reducing report reliability.
actions: Replaced bare division with CAST(AS DOUBLE) + NULLIF pattern to eliminate integer truncation across all ratio metrics
Defined a prefix-based naming convention (raw_ / u_ / m_ / sum_) to prevent alias-vs-source column collisions; documented and rolled out to team
Joined amazon_attributed_events_by_traffic_time (KPI) and sponsored_ads_traffic (spend) via FULL OUTER JOIN to capture campaigns with conversion data only or spend data only
Ported Google Sheets campaign classification logic into SQL CASE statements covering SD/PD split, Dog/Cat segment, 4-level subfunnel, and top-level funnel (Awareness / Consideration / Purchase)
Built a standalone QA query to surface unclassified campaigns before reporting, establishing a self-verification loop
Designed queries with AMC privacy aggregation thresholds (LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH / VERY_HIGH) in mind
impact: All Sponsored Ads campaigns now report KPI (revenue, NTB revenue, orders, NTB orders, AOV, NTB rate, units) alongside media cost in a single output table
Removed root cause of ratio metric errors, improving trustworthiness of performance reports
Delivered a reusable AMC SQL template and a 7-step debugging checklist adopted as team standard

headline: AMC Overlap Analysis Revealed Retargeting Gap Across DSP and Sponsored Ads
context: Built an end-to-end AMC audience overlap pipeline for a pet nutrition brand's Japan awareness campaigns; sole data engineer and analyst on the project.

detail.overview: Designed and delivered a cross-channel audience overlap analysis inside Amazon Marketing Cloud for a pet nutrition brand running awareness campaigns in Japan. Covered Amazon DSP (FTV, PVA, B+, Video, DSP_Static) and Sponsored Ads, and produced client-ready visualizations to guide media reallocation.
detail.problem: The team needed to know whether upper-funnel DSP channels were reaching audiences that later got retargeted by the primary conversion channel (DSP_Static), and how much SA and DSP Awareness audiences overlapped — without individual-level data exposure.
actions: Authored privacy-compliant AMC SQL: user_id never surfaced in final SELECT; COUNT(DISTINCT) scoped to CTEs only.
Parsed campaign names (LOWER + LIKE) to classify five funnel subgroups: FTV, PVA, B+, Video, DSP_Static, plus SA Awareness.
Engineered pairwise and union overlap matrices via self-joins to work around AMC's lack of window frame support; calculated directional coverage % (within-base and of-union).
Diagnosed a data-window pitfall where staggered flight dates risked a false 'zero overlap' reading; validated that set-based overlap logic is unaffected and corrected the methodology.
Built brand-guideline-compliant Python visualizations: stacked bar (reach vs. retargeted), pairwise heatmap/network graph, and 2-set Venn diagrams exported as transparent PNG/SVG for PPT.
impact: Quantified that FTV (51%) and PVA (44%) had the weakest overlap with DSP_Static, flagging a likely conversion bottleneck where upper-funnel audiences exit without retargeting.
Showed 81% (SD) / 72% (PD) of SA Awareness reach was already covered by DSP Awareness, surfacing potential frequency duplication.
Delivered actionable reallocation rationale grounded in clean-room measurement rather than modeled estimates.

headline: AMC Frequency Analysis Surfaces Optimal Ad Caps Across Full Purchase Funnel
context: Ran funnel-stage frequency analyses in Amazon Marketing Cloud for a pet nutrition brand's Japan market, covering Awareness through Loyalty, to identify where ad exposure stops driving incremental value.

detail.overview: Used AMC SQL and Python to build a frequency–outcome analysis across four funnel stages (Awareness, Consideration, Purchase, Loyalty) for two product lines. Goal was to find the frequency point at which additional impressions stop converting, and to make a budget reallocation case toward new-user acquisition.
detail.problem: The brand was running without empirically validated frequency caps per funnel stage. Over-exposure to existing and loyal customers risked cannibalizing organic conversions and wasting budget that could go toward prospecting.
actions: Defined stage-specific KPIs: reach efficiency (Awareness), CTR/DPVR (Consideration), purchase rate (Purchase), Subscribe & Save rate (Loyalty).
Cleaned time windows by excluding promotional periods and attribution-incomplete weeks; used advertiser-timezone date fields throughout.
Worked around AMC SQL dialect constraints — no window-frame functions, no ORDER BY — by rewriting cumulative and incremental calculations as self-joins.
Flagged and corrected analytical pitfalls including selection bias and volume-driven dilution at the frequency cap.
Built reusable Python visualization templates (transparent PNG/SVG, brand palette, PPT-optimized) and an AMC SQL best-practice ruleset.
impact: Awareness: 70% cumulative reach validated at frequency 2 — confirmed the global 2x/7-day guideline with local Japan data.
Consideration: efficiency peaks at 1–2 impressions; the FQ3 dip is volume concentration at the cap, not ad fatigue.
Purchase (Prescription Diet): diminishing returns clear beyond ~7 impressions; 90%+ of buyers were already ad-exposed, flagging cannibalization risk.
Loyalty: Subscribe & Save rate declines monotonically with frequency; ~60% of subscribers convert organically — strongest evidence that high frequency does not drive incremental subscriptions.
Delivered a unified strategic recommendation to reduce over-exposure and shift budget from existing/loyal customers toward new-user acquisition.

headline: Built AMC SQL Pipelines for Cross-Channel KPI Measurement and Audience Overlap Analysis
context: AMC Data Engineer/Analyst supporting campaign measurement and audience analytics across Amazon DSP and Sponsored Ads for agency clients

detail.overview: Designed, built, and debugged Amazon Marketing Cloud SQL pipelines delivering unified KPI dashboards and cross-channel audience overlap insights. Also handled data quality fixes, feasibility consulting on AMC data boundaries, and bilingual technical client communication.
detail.problem: Clients needed reliable cross-channel measurement (SA + DSP) in AMC, but existing queries had data accuracy issues and lacked reach-deduplication visibility. One Japanese-language client also needed verified answers on AMC audience lifecycle mechanics.
actions: Built unified KPI query covering impressions, clicks, NTB sales, CTR/CPC/CPM across Sponsored Ads and DSP using CTE-based pipeline
Fixed integer-division truncation bug via explicit CAST AS DOUBLE and resolved NULL contamination from LEFT JOINs using COALESCE
Designed cross-channel unique-user overlap analysis (DSP Awareness × AMS General) using UNION ALL and self-join to produce reach-deduplication matrix
Assessed feasibility of extracting DSP line-item targeting config from AMC; clarified the boundary between configuration data and event-level delivered data, and proposed API/upload alternatives
Standardized campaign × line-item master list query with reach metrics, respecting AMC aggregation thresholds
Verified AMC audience lifecycle mechanics (refreshRateDays, 100-day inactivity rule) against official documentation and drafted accurate technical response in Japanese for a JP client
impact: Delivered production-ready unified KPI dashboard query covering full-funnel metrics for 2026 trend analysis
Resolved data accuracy issues in cost and derived metrics calculations
Provided clients with cross-channel reach-overlap visibility to support media-mix decisions
Clarified AMC clean-room data boundaries to prevent mis-scoped measurement requests and unblocked alternative solutions

headline: Diagnosed AMC Data Gaps and Delivered Actionable Workarounds for Two Brands in One Day
context: AMC Data Analyst supporting a global PC brand and a global pet nutrition brand — ran three parallel analyses in a single session covering off-Amazon conversion categorization, B2B/B2C audience segmentation feasibility, and campaign audience overlap diagnosis
metrics: 1,573 off-Amazon purchases bucketed by price proxy
35% audience overlap root-caused to 3 structural setting errors

detail.overview: Three AMC analysis requests from two clients (a global PC brand and a global pet nutrition brand) completed on the same date. Each task required diagnosing a data limitation, designing a workaround within AMC constraints, and communicating findings clearly to the client.
detail.problem: (1) The PC brand's off-Amazon conversion events lacked product-type metadata, making PC vs. accessory distinction impossible. (2) The PC brand asked whether AMC could distinguish Business vs. Consumer purchasers. (3) The pet nutrition brand's Dog and Cat Awareness campaigns showed 35% audience overlap with no clear cause.
actions: T1 — Used off_amazon_product_sales as a price proxy; wrote AMC SQL to bucket 1,573 conversions into 7 price tiers; visualized distribution on a log scale to surface high-value (¥80k+) concentration likely attributable to PC units.

T2 — Audited is_amazon_business field limitations (SP/SB only, no off-Amazon support); designed a conversion_event_name tag-based segmentation approach; identified pixel vs. CAPI double-count risk on C-prefix parallel tags and resolved via source filtering; documented privacy aggregation threshold constraints. Delivered findings in Japanese.

T3 — Conducted a segment-ID-level audit of both campaigns; found shared 'Pet Owners' segment IDs across Dog and Cat, plus Dog IDs incorrectly included in the Cat list; classified overlap causes into four categories with a contribution vs. resolvability matrix.
impact: Gave the PC brand a practical path to approximate product-category attribution despite missing metadata, with 1,573 purchases distributed across price tiers.
Clarified the hard limits of AMC's B2B/B2C detection and provided two concrete alternatives (split tag firing, 1P data upload), preventing wasted engineering effort on an unsupported use case.
Separated structural overlap (3 setting-level causes, addressable) from natural overlap (multi-pet households, inherent) for the pet nutrition brand, giving the team a prioritized fix list.

headline: Audited and Standardized Amazon Sponsored Ads Creative Specs Across 4 Ad Formats
context: Cross-verified draft creative specifications against three official Amazon sources (Seller Central, Amazon Ads, Advertising API) and consolidated findings into a single reference table for the team

detail.overview: Built a verified creative specification guide covering Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, SP Video, and SB Video — correcting errors in an existing draft and structuring all rules into one unified table.
detail.problem: The team's draft spec document contained multiple inaccuracies (audio requirements, file-size limits, aspect ratios), creating risk of ad rejection during campaign setup.
actions: Cross-referenced draft specs against Seller Central G1881, advertising.amazon.com/resources/ad-specs, and Advertising API documentation
Identified and corrected key errors: SP Video audio spec, SB Custom Image 5 MB vs. Logo 1 MB distinction, added SB Video 9:16 support, fixed aspect-ratio and file-size figures
Structured all ad-type rules (size, format, file size, landing-page requirements) into a single bilingual comparison table for internal use
impact: Reduced creative rejection risk by catching spec errors before campaign launch
Gave the team a single source of truth for Amazon ad creative production [검증필요: 실제 팀 활용 범위 확인]

headline: Built AMC Retargeting Audiences for a Global PC Brand's Portfolio (402 ASINs)
context: Designed and activated privacy-safe retargeting audiences in Amazon Marketing Cloud clean room, targeting a global PC brand's product line across Amazon DSP and Sponsored Ads

detail.overview: Built retargeting audiences in Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC) clean room for a global PC brand's portfolio of 402 ASINs, segmenting users by cart-add-without-purchase and SA-click-without-purchase behavior across 90/60/30-day recency windows.
detail.problem: The brand needed to re-engage high-intent users who added products to cart or clicked Sponsored Ads but did not complete a purchase. No structured audience segments existed for these behaviors at ASIN level.
actions: Standardized 402 ASINs into a reusable CTE (VALUES) block with deduplication and validity checks
Applied anti-join logic on unified organic + ad purchase data (FSI conversions_all) to isolate non-purchasers
Designed SA-click audience using creative_asin from sponsored_ads_traffic; pre-validated SP/SB data coverage limits
Wrote ASIN-level exploratory queries to confirm sales and add-to-cart data availability before audience build
Ensured all queries comply with AMC SQL constraints: no window frames, no RIGHT JOIN, aggregation thresholds, user_id privacy rules
impact: Delivered 3 cart-add-without-purchase audiences (90/60/30-day) ready for DSP activation
SA-click-without-purchase audiences (90/60/30-day) in progress
Established reusable, standardized query templates for future AMC audience builds

headline: Built 10 NTB Audience Segments in AMC Clean Room for a Global PC Brand's Japan Market
context: Designed and deployed rule-based AMC audiences via SQL to support a New-to-Brand customer acquisition campaign for a global PC brand's Japan market; acted as AMC Data Engineer and Data Analyst under a media agency.

detail.overview: Built 10 rule-based audience segments inside Amazon Marketing Cloud to identify high-intent, never-purchased shoppers for the brand's NTB acquisition campaign. Segments covered 300+ ASINs across two intent signals and five lookback windows.
detail.problem: The brand needed to reach genuinely new customers without retargeting past buyers. Standard DSP audiences couldn't enforce Amazon's 12-month NTB definition at the signal level, and Japan-timezone boundary errors risked miscounting eligible users.
actions: Designed two audience categories — Add-to-Cart non-purchasers (conversions_all / FSI) and Sponsored Ads click non-purchasers (sponsored_ads_traffic) — each across 30 / 60 / 90 / 180 / 365-day lookback windows, totalling 10 segments.
Fixed the purchase-exclusion window at 365 days to align with Amazon's official NTB definition, decoupling it from the shorter intent windows to avoid mis-qualifying returning buyers.
Converted UTC-native AMC columns to JST (Asia/Tokyo) via CONVERT_TIME_ZONE_FROM_UTC to ensure accurate period boundaries for the advertiser's local timezone.
Applied a two-stage filter — coarse sargable prune first, then precise TZ boundary check — to keep query performance within AMC execution limits.
Mapped all 300+ brand ASINs to the target list and documented audience logic in Japanese for client-side review.
impact: 30-day and 60-day segments failed to meet AMC's minimum user threshold, surfacing a reach ceiling for short-window intent signals in this category.
Recommended a delivery-rate vs. purchase-intent trade-off strategy using the viable 90 / 180 / 365-day segments, giving the client a phased audience activation plan.

headline: Run Amazon Marketing Cloud (AMC) analytics end-to-end at a global agency
context: Data-engineering + data-science analyst at WPP Media Japan — from raw clean-room SQL to client-facing insight and presentation.

detail.overview: Analyst on WPP Media Japan's Amazon commerce team, owning measurement and analytics end-to-end for global advertisers.
detail.problem: Raw clean-room signals (AMC) have to be turned into validated, decision-ready insight and communicated to clients — a mix of data engineering, analysis, and consulting.
actions: Build AMC queries from scratch and QA them for errors before running
Validate the returned data, then analyze and visualize it
Pressure-test insights internally, then translate them into optimization actions with the media team
Build client-facing slides, write the talk track, and present
impact: Turns Amazon clean-room measurement into optimization decisions and client-ready narratives
Owns the full measurement loop: build → validate → insight → action → present

headline: Turned around a premium whisky brand's Amazon DSP with disciplined A/B testing
context: ROAS was sliding below 1.0 with lower-funnel delivery stuck at 20-40%; recovered it by testing every lever one by one.
metrics: Balanced spend across full ACPL funnel

detail.overview: A premium single-malt whisky client on Amazon DSP was losing performance through 2026 — ROAS below 1.0 and lower-funnel line items delivering at only 20-40% due to tiny audience pools and up to 99% audience mismatch.
detail.problem: Recover ROAS above 1.0 and fix delivery without breaking the funnel's budget balance.
actions: Broke the problem into levers and A/B tested each independently
Expanded audiences across the funnel via AMC custom audiences — the single biggest, fastest lever, especially for lower funnels
Split the awareness line by audience category (whisky vs. travel) for cleaner measurement and budget control
Replaced Amazon's automated budget allocation with a projected-spend cap to stop overspend on weak lines
Swapped underperforming affinity audiences for competitor keyword/ASIN targeting, sourced via a self-built web-scraping automation, with a renewal cadence
Turned off non-contributing offsite placements and diversified creative
impact: Lower-funnel delivery improved from 20-40% to 90-99%
ROAS recovered from below 1.0 to consistently above 1.0
Balanced spend across the whole ACPL funnel

headline: Built an ACPL funnel penetration analysis to reallocate budget by reach
context: Client wanted to know which funnel campaigns had already maxed out reach so budget could shift to under-penetrated funnels.

detail.overview: A pet-nutrition client asked for a penetration analysis across the ACPL funnel to identify saturated campaigns.
detail.problem: Measure audience penetration and saturation per funnel — including for custom line-item audiences not in Amazon's standard segment catalog.
actions: Built AMC queries to compute penetration over Amazon's owned audience segments
Tackled the harder problem of estimating reachable audience size for custom competitor-signal line items
Used the findings to inform a Sponsored Products restructure that cut wasted spend
impact: Gave the client a reach-based view to move budget from saturated funnels to under-penetrated ones

headline: Split a blended audience to fix ad efficiency (audience dilution)
context: Whisky + Travel ran as one blended DSP line; splitting them let the algorithm measure and bid on each independently.

detail.overview: On Amazon DSP, whisky is a small audience, so — like most competitors — it was paired with a related lifestyle audience (Travel) in one blended line for scale.
detail.problem: From early 2026 the blended line lost efficiency: same or higher cost, fewer clicks, lower ROAS. Hypothesis: the two related audiences were competing for the algorithm's attention rather than reinforcing it (audience dilution).
actions: Formed the audience-dilution hypothesis from the cost/click/ROAS pattern
Split the single blended line into two independent lines in June
Weighted most budget to Whisky as the priority audience, ran Travel independently
Reframed Travel toward awareness KPIs (CTR/DPVR) since it converts weakly
impact: Cost down 37%
Clicks up 78%
ROAS improved from 0.5 to 0.94 (+88%)
Sales up 19%
